briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 10, 2026.

sections.title: 🌱 Strengthening Climate Action by Domestic Companies and Institutions
sections.content: According to news released today, domestic companies' efforts to address climate change are showing tangible results. Among companies participating in the CDP (Carbon Disclosure Project), 91% are managing climate change at the board level, and major companies such as KOGAS, LG Innotek, KB, and Shinhan have earned leadership ratings, demonstrating their competitiveness in carbon management [1, 2, 4, 5]. Furthermore, Sinan-gun and Jeju Island are expanding carbon sinks through tree-planting events [6, 13], and Hyundai Department Store has donated trees to carbon-neutral schools, supporting local communities' carbon-neutral practices [7]. Sangji University is extending the operation of the Wonju Carbon Neutral Support Center, continuing its role as a focal point for the community's transition to carbon neutrality, and Jecheon City plans to operate a carbon-neutral points system starting in 2026 to reward citizens for their energy-saving practices [8, 11, 16]. These movements demonstrate the active participation of businesses and local communities in responding to climate change.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Government's Response to Climate Crisis and Acceleration of Energy Transition
sections.content: In preparation for an era of industrial transition towards AI and carbon neutrality, the government is pursuing the establishment of a 'Basic Plan for Employment Stability during Industrial Transition,' proactively addressing public concerns about job changes [9, 14, 15]. Administrator Lee Mi-sun of the Korea Meteorological Administration emphasized the importance of securing technologies to protect citizens and disclosing data in the era of climate crisis [3]. With an energy self-sufficiency rate of approximately 15%, South Korea views the expansion of renewable energy as a key solution [18], and Jeollanam-do Province is collaborating with related organizations, including Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO), to accelerate the public-led creation of renewable energy generation complexes [17, 20, 25, 26]. Just as Sweden is exploring a mix of nuclear and wind power as a solution for carbon neutrality [10], South Korea is also considering various energy mix strategies, and discussions are underway in rural areas regarding whether 'Sunshine Income,' a renewable energy profit-sharing model, could become a new income solution [19].

sections.title: ⚠️ Accelerating Global Warming and Widespread Threats
sections.content: Recent research findings have for the first time confirmed that global warming has accelerated over the past decade, once again highlighting the severity of the climate crisis [40]. These changes are having widespread global impacts, with allergy seasons lengthening in North Carolina [33], and extreme heat threatening coffee production, one of the world's most beloved beverages [35]. Furthermore, new drought-prone areas are emerging even in regions experiencing increased rainfall [47], and research indicating that one-third of the global population resides in areas where activities are severely restricted due to extreme heat demonstrates that climate change is no longer a distant future problem [56]. Island nations like Polynesia are at risk of being submerged and disappearing due to climate change [50], and there are also reports from Tanzania that climate change is reshaping disease maps [54], further increasing the urgency for immediate action.

sections.title: 🔴 Regressive Movements Hindering Climate Action and Safety Issues
sections.content: Amidst efforts to address the climate crisis, concerning news continues to emerge. The Florida House of Representatives passed a bill that curbs local climate action, demonstrating a regressive stance against climate change response [43]. Domestically, 38 fatalities have occurred over the past six years at renewable energy facility installation and maintenance sites, issuing a serious warning about safety complacency [23]. Furthermore, it was revealed that Mercedes-Benz equipped its electric vehicles with low-cost battery cells and engaged in false advertising, resulting in a fine from the Fair Trade Commission and a complaint filed with the prosecution [27]; this emphasizes the importance of ethical responsibility for companies promoting eco-friendly products. In Australia, a report suggesting that the climate crisis will exacerbate national security threats has been classified and its contents not disclosed [57], highlighting the need for vigilance against movements that hinder climate action or lack transparency.

briefing.closing: This concludes today's climate briefing. Responding to climate change requires the continuous attention and effort of us all. We look forward to seeing you at the next briefing.

articles.title: Mercedes-Benz Sold EVs with Low-Cost Batteries at High Prices... FTC Imposes 11.2 Billion KRW Fine and Files Complaint with Prosecutors
articles.original_title: 전기차에 싸구려 배터리 탑재하고 고가에 판 벤츠…공정위, 112억 과징금·검찰 고발
articles.description: First sanction for omitting/concealing battery information. The Fair Trade Commission imposed a fine of over 10 billion KRW on Mercedes-Benz for falsely advertising that its electric vehicles were equipped with expensive, world-leading batteries, despite using low-cost batteries. The FTC stated that Mercedes-Benz concealed the fact that many EQE and EQS EV models were equipped with Farasis battery cells, making it appear as if all EVs were equipped with products from CATL, the world's leading battery cell manufacturer.

summary.phenomenon: Mercedes-Benz falsely advertised that all its electric vehicles, including the EQE and EQS models, were equipped with world-leading battery cells, despite having installed low-cost battery cells in them. Consequently, the Fair Trade Commission imposed a fine of 11.2 billion KRW on Mercedes-Benz and filed a complaint with prosecutors.
summary.cause: This is because Mercedes-Benz deceived consumers by omitting and concealing electric vehicle battery information.

articles.title: Hyundai Mobis Establishes Fourth European Production Base in Hungary
articles.original_title: 현대모비스, 헝가리에 4번째 유럽 생산거점
articles.description: Supplying EV chassis modules to Mercedes-Benz. Hyundai Mobis announced on the 10th that it recently signed a chassis module supply contract with Mercedes-Benz and began operating a dedicated production base in Hungary. Hyundai Mobis, which has been supplying chassis modules to Mercedes-Benz through its Alabama plant in the U.S. since 2022, has now successfully secured orders for the same components in Europe. Chassis modules integrate braking, steering, and suspension components for the vehicle's undercarriage, serving client companies...

summary.phenomenon: Hyundai Mobis has signed a supply contract for electric vehicle chassis modules with Mercedes-Benz and has begun operating its fourth European production base in Hungary. Hyundai Mobis has been supplying chassis modules to Mercedes-Benz through its Alabama plant in the U.S. since 2022.
